summary: No-ship: the provider-launch path is only wired through the fallback PTY wrapper, and explicit `primary_runtime: claude-code-runtime` still silently falls back when that runtime is not actually composed.

findings.title: Primary provider overrides are not carried through the normal gateway launch path
findings.recommendation: Thread `primaryProviderLaunch` into the actual `OperativePtySession.spawn(...)` call used by the gateway path, not just into child-process env. In this slice, `GARRISON_PROVIDER_LAUNCH` is only consumed by [scripts/pty-operative.mjs], which is used only by `spawnClaude` fallback, so compositions that select a gateway fitting will likely drop the intended `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` keep-behavior.

findings.title: Explicitly naming the default primary runtime still silently falls back when that runtime is not composed
findings.recommendation: Only synthesize the implicit default when `primary_runtime` is unset/blank. If the user explicitly sets `primary_runtime: claude-code-runtime` and that fitting is not composed, throw like the other uncomposed-primary cases. Add a unit test for the explicit-default-id-but-not-composed scenario.
